shot: 03 — Before forests. Before apples. Before squirrels had anywhere to hide nuts.
rationale: Best current continuation of the v15/v16 premium teal museum-stage style.
Avoids the big v18/v19 failure: no real forest backdrop, no obvious trees/plants/grass/leaves.
Apple/red fruit is clear and mobile-readable; squirrel read is clear; stage-camera motion would work well.
Distinct from Shots 1 and 2 while preserving lighting/material language.
caveat: Still not a final lock: nut/acorn is not clear enough and there are multiple squirrel-like figures. If we refine, use v20_05 as style/composition target but force one squirrel and a larger separate brown nut/acorn.
rejected_lessons: Using tree/forest/branch/leaf words in the positive prompt instantly generated real forest scenes, even when the intent was ghost/absence icons.
Replacing apple with “red round fruit” reduced leaves but the model still over-indexed on squirrel characters.
For this line, a premium stage/proof gag is better than any outdoor prompt.
motion_note: Use a subtle stage-camera slide/push, dust motes, and spotlight drift; do not I2V-animate the squirrel unless it stays pristine.
